key_insight: The paper introduces a fully model-free deep learning framework for high-dimensional nonlinear parabolic PDEs that estimates gradients and Hessians via zeroth-order simulations instead of repeated automatic differentiation.
engineering_relevance: This makes PDE solving more robust in black-box or data-driven environments such as continuous-time reinforcement learning, where the dynamics are unknown but simulation and pointwise queries are available.
method: Use two simulator types as data-generating mechanisms and learn the solution together with its derivatives in a represent-then-learn pipeline.
Construct zeroth-order derivative (ZOD) estimators from perturbed Monte Carlo trajectories to produce training targets for gradient and Hessian networks.
Prove a non-asymptotic error bound with explicit contributions from discretization, approximation, statistical error, and ZOD bias.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments showing competitive performance in moderate and high dimensions, alongside a theoretical sample-complexity analysis in weighted Sobolev space.
watch_item: ZOD estimators introduce a bias-variance tradeoff, so performance depends on perturbation design, simulation quality, and the contraction assumption holding in practice.
paper_title: A Zeroth-Order Deep Learning Method for Fully Nonlinear Parabolic Partial Differential Equations with Unknown Coefficients
